qz_26a199a4dc.topic: Explain solving linear equations and inequalities

questions.question_text: What is the first step in solving a linear equation?
choices: Add the same value to both sides of the equation
Subtract the same value from both sides of the equation
Multiply both sides of the equation by the same value
Divide both sides of the equation by the same value

questions.question_text: What is the purpose of isolating the variable in a linear equation?
choices: To make the equation more complex

questions.question_text: What is the difference between a linear equation and a linear inequality?
choices: A linear equation has a variable on both sides, while a linear inequality has a variable on one side
A linear equation has a variable on one side, while a linear inequality has a variable on both sides
A linear equation has a constant on both sides, while a linear inequality has a constant on one side
A linear equation has a variable on one side, while a linear inequality has a variable on one side

questions.question_text: How do you solve a linear inequality?
choices: By adding or subtracting the same value from both sides
By multiplying or dividing both sides by the same value
By isolating the variable on one side of the inequality
By graphing the inequality on a number line

questions.question_text: What is the concept of 'like terms' in linear equations?
choices: Terms that have the same variable and coefficient
Terms that have the same variable but different coefficients
Terms that have different variables but the same coefficient
Terms that have different variables and coefficients

questions.question_text: How do you simplify a linear equation?
What is the purpose of using inverse operations in linear equations?
How do you check the solution to a linear equation?
choices: By plugging the solution back into the original equation

questions.question_text: What is the concept of 'solving for the variable' in linear equations?
choices: Finding the value of the variable that makes the equation true
Finding the value of the variable that makes the equation false
Finding the value of the variable that makes the inequality true
Finding the value of the variable that makes the inequality false

questions.question_text: How do you graph a linear equation?
choices: By plotting two points on the coordinate plane
By using a ruler and pencil

questions.question_text: What is the method used for measuring surface area?
choices: Measuring the length and width of the surface and then finding the product of these measurements
Measuring the area of the surface directly

Measuring the perimeter of the surface

choices: The number of pieces is equal to the product of the length and width
The number of pieces is greater than the product of the length and width
The number of pieces is less than the product of the length and width
The number of pieces is not related to the product of the length and width

choices: It makes numbers shorter and easier to write
It makes numbers longer and harder to write

choices: It makes numbers longer and harder to write
It makes numbers more difficult to read
It makes numbers more prone to errors

qz_04ccc9b824.topic: Explanation of the first lesson in physics

questions.question_text: What is the main topic of the first lesson in physics?
What type of motion is studied in the first lesson?
What is the main concept that is extended in the first lesson?
What is the unit outcome of the first lesson?
choices: Understand concepts related to force and uniform motion
Develop skill of manipulating problems related to statics and uniform motion
Appreciate the interrelatedness of all things
Use a wide range of possibilities for developing knowledge of the major concepts within physics

questions.question_text: Who said 'A moving object has a natural tendency to come to rest'?
What is the title of the first unit?
What is the main concept that is studied in the first unit?
What is the main idea of the first lesson?
choices: To learn about motion in one dimension
To learn about pressure and its effects
To learn about measurement and its importance
To learn about force and its effects

questions.question_text: What is the unit outcome of the first unit?
choices: Develop skill of manipulating problems related to pressure
Appreciate the interrelatedness of all things
Use a wide range of possibilities for developing knowledge of the major concepts within physics

questions.question_text: What is the main concept that is developed in the first unit?
choices: Skill of manipulating problems related to statics and uniform motion
Skill of manipulating problems related to pressure
Skill of measuring area, volume and density
Skill of producing and evaluation of a design project

questions.question_text: What type of motion is characterized by a constant speed?
What does the slope of the v against t graph represent?
What is the area under the v against t graph equal to?
What is the formula for average speed?
What is the total displacement for the 4 hours in the given scenario?
What is the distance traveled by the car in the given scenario?
What is the slope of the s against t graph equal to?
What is the formula for the slope of the s against t graph?
What type of motion is represented by a straight line graph in the v against t graph?
What is the net displacement for the 4 hours in the given scenario?
What is a pyramid?
choices: A solid figure formed by line segments joining every point on the sides and every interior points of a polygonal region with a point outside of the plane of the polygon.
A type of triangle with three equal sides.
A type of quadrilateral with four equal sides.
A type of polygon with five equal sides.

questions.question_text: What is the name of the vertex of the pyramid?
What is the name of the base of the pyramid?
What is the name of the lateral faces of the pyramid?
What is the name of the height of the pyramid?
What is the name of the base edge of the pyramid?
What is the name of the lateral edge of the pyramid?
What is a regular pyramid?
choices: A pyramid with a square base and four equal sides.
A pyramid with a triangular base and three equal sides.
A pyramid with a regular polygon as its base and all lateral edges of equal length.
A pyramid with a quadrilateral base and four equal sides.

questions.question_text: What is the altitude of a pyramid?
choices: The perpendicular distance from the vertex to the point of the base.
The length of the altitude of a lateral face of the pyramid.
The length of the base of the pyramid.
The length of the lateral edge of the pyramid.

questions.question_text: What is the slant height of a pyramid?
choices: The length of the altitude of a lateral face of the pyramid.
The length of the base of the pyramid.
The length of the lateral edge of the pyramid.
The length of the altitude of the pyramid.
